name: Colistin
indications:
## Main indications

Multidrug-resistant organisms, cystic fibrosis.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial containing 100 mg colistin base (Permalec*, Fabra*, Colistina Richet*, Techsphere*, Alfacolin*, Alficetin, Nolisin, Cotrelan*, Colislym, Espirotech*).
adulto.reconstitution: 2 mL sterile water for injection. Conc: 50 mg/mL.
adulto.diluent: 100 mg in 50-100 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Reconstitute in 3-5 mL sterile water for injection and administer over 3-5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 50 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose and infuse over 10-15 min.
adulto.notes: 100 mg colistin base is approximately equivalent to 240 mg colistin methanesulfonate and 3.000.000 IU potency. IM or inhalation administration is possible.

pediatrico.diluent: 0.9% NaCl, 5% dextrose, sterile water for injection, Ringer solution.
pediatrico.dose: IV: 2,5 colistin base/kg/dose every 12 h. Severe infections, critically ill patients, and cystic fibrosis: 7 mg colistin base/kg/day every 8 h. Maximum dose: 100 mg/dose. Adults loading dose: 5 mg colistin base/kg/dose (maximum dose 300 mg); then 12 h post loading: 100 mg/dose every 8 h. Equivalence: 1 mg sodium colistimethate = 12.500 U sodium colistimethate = 0,4 mg colistin base.
pediatrico.compatibility: Precipitates concomitantly with erythromycin, cephalothin, tetracycline.
pediatrico.notes: Increases risk of ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity with aminoglycosides, cyclosporine, amphotericin, and cisplatin.

neonatal.dose: Dose per NICU protocol and weight; strict renal monitoring.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 24 h at room temperature, 48 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- Use immediately once diluted. (24 h at room temperature).

## Pediatric guide

- 8 h at room temperature, 24 h refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Muscle weakness, facial paresthesia, visual disturbances, nephrotoxic. May cause bronchospasm when nebulized (treat with β-agonists).
